alignments.candidate_evidence: Designs, specs, and directs LLM coding agents to build production systems; owns evaluation methodology, benchmarking, and calibration. Eight years at Google with deep AI and agentic tooling experience.
alignments.job_requirement: 3+ years of experience in ML evaluation, benchmarking for LLM or agentic systems

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations leader with demonstrated capacity to own AI/agentic-tooling evaluation methodology and benchmarking; capable of designing and directing complex ML systems.
alignments.job_requirement: Deep understanding of concepts and algorithms underlying machine learning and LLMs

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years in Strategy and Operations roles at scale; demonstrated stakeholder management and cross-functional communication at Google across products and transformations.
alignments.job_requirement: Ability to explain complex technical concepts in simple terms with technical and non-technical audiences

gaps.gap: Strong technical coding skills in Python (required). Candidate states basic Python, actively learning data-analysis/pandas track.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has demonstrated ability to learn and scale technical capabilities; owns agentic-tooling evaluation. Python basics with focused pandas/data-analysis learning may suffice for evaluation infrastructure if not blocked by hiring team's interpretation of 'strong.'  However, this is a stated requirement for an ML evaluation engineering role.

gaps.gap: Production ML engineering background. Candidate's ML evaluation work is design/direction/methodology-focused rather than hands-on engineering implementation.
gaps.mitigation: None. Role requires building scalable evaluation pipelines and infrastructure; candidate has no stated production software-development or ML-engineering background. Honest gap: 'No production software-engineering background.'

gaps.gap: Open-source ML framework contributions or published research (nice-to-have). Candidate has no stated history.
gaps.mitigation: Stated as 'ideally' not required. Not a hard blocker.

gaps.gap: ML frameworks (PyTorch, HuggingFace Transformers). No evidence supplied.
gaps.mitigation: Stated as 'ideally' preferred. Candidate owns AI/agentic evaluation methodology but no stated hands-on framework experience.

hard_blockers.evidence: Strong technical coding skills in Python" (required, in "About you" section). Candidate profile states "Python: basic, actively learning (data-analysis / pandas track)." Role is an ML evaluation engineering position requiring production-grade evaluation infrastructure and pipelines; basic Python is below the threshold for a hands-on engineering build role.

output.level_rationale: Role requires 3+ years in ML evaluation/benchmarking plus proven hands-on implementation and strong Python. independently responsible for designing evaluation infrastructure and frameworks across customer verticals. Reports to Applied AI leadership (implied). cross-functional ownership of research-to-production pipeline. Estimated L5 IC or early-lead scope. Company stage (founded ~2023, Series B-C inferred) and role positioning as 'first' in this function suggest senior IC expectations. Confidence reduced due to Paris-based role and absence of explicit salary or team-size context in JD.".
output.summary: Evaluation Engineer is a specialized ML engineering role requiring strong hands-on Python, production infrastructure-build experience, and 3+ years in ML evaluation/benchmarking—outside the candidate's core Strategy and Operations, Product Operations, or transformation-program families. Candidate excels in AI/agentic-tooling evaluation methodology and direction but has no production software-engineering background and only basic Python. The hard blocker (strong Python required vs. basic + learning) and mismatch in engineering scope recommend skipping this role despite strong methodology and stakeholder-management foundations.
uncertainties: Mistral AI company size and maturity beyond inferred Series B-C; impact on scope and team structure unclear.
Whether 'strong technical coding skills in Python' is a strict gate or negotiable for a methodology-forward evaluation leader.
Depth of candidate's hands-on agentic-tooling evaluation work (design/direction/methodology vs. actual pipeline/infrastructure implementation).
Whether candidate's BigQuery, SQL, Looker, and Tableau skills could partially compensate for Python baseline in evaluation infrastructure roles (likely not for this role).
